problem.title: Laning Phase
problem.description:
The game is played on a strip of n squares numbered from 1 to n. Kiaya starts at position x and Zeros at position y (1 ≤ x < y ≤ n). Players alternate turns, Kiaya moves first. On his turn a player at position p must choose a non-zero integer k such that:
• -a ≤ k ≤ a for Kiaya, -b ≤ k ≤ b for Zeros;
• 1 ≤ p + k ≤ n
and moves to p+k. If after a move Kiaya’s position becomes greater than or equal to Zeros’s position, the mover wins (the opponent is forced to dash into or over him). Both play optimally. For each test case determine which player has a winning strategy or if the result is a draw.
Input: The first line contains τ (1 ≤ τ ≤ 10^5), the number of test cases. Each of the next τ lines contains five integers n, x, y, a, b (2 ≤ n ≤ 10^9, 1 ≤ x < y ≤ n, 1 ≤ a, b < n).
Output: For each test case output one line: "Kiaya" if Kiaya can force a win, "Zeros" if Zeros can force a win, or "Draw" if neither can force a win.
Sample Input:
3
3 2 3 2 1
4 3 4 2 1
999 1 999 1 1
Sample Output:
Zeros
Kiaya
Kiaya
problem.input_format:
The first line contains an integer τ (1 ≤ τ ≤ 10^5), the number of test cases.
Each of the next τ lines contains five space‑separated integers n, x, y, a, b.
Constraints are: 2 ≤ n ≤ 10^9, 1 ≤ x < y ≤ n, 1 ≤ a, b < n.
problem.output_format:
For each test case output a single line containing one of the strings:
Kiaya
Zeros
Draw

all_testcases.description: Official sample test covering basic scenarios and a large distance with minimal moves
Edge cases: minimum board size, symmetric max moves, Kiaya with larger step range, and mixed step ranges near board limits
Medium - Kiaya has immediate winning move (a large enough)
Medium - Zeros can force win on his first turn (b small enough)
Medium - Expected draw with equal small moves
Medium - General case with moderate ranges

warnings: Khong co KILLER cases de kiem tra do kho.
